name: devious

metadata.name: devious
metadata.summary: Generic development cli for various application types.

documentation:
# What is Devious?

Devious is a command line utility to manage the lifecycle of applications in a mono repository.
It is developed in the context of a VSCode-based development container template (see [devious](https://github.com/flxtrtwn/devious)).

# What can I do with Devious?

-   `create`, `build`, `test`, `debug` applications locally
-   `deploy`, `run` and `stop` applications (e.g. on remote machines)
-   Manage remote machines and `install` infrastructure

# What kind of applications does Devious support?

Devious features an extensible target model and can provide functionality for any application type that implements the `Target` interface.
It currently supports `microservice` and `django_app` targets and can manage their lifecycle.

functions.name: devious.utils.wait_for_callable
documentation: Execute code while waiting for a Process based on a Callable to complete.

functions.name: devious.utils.temp_env
documentation: Temporary set environment variables, e.g. for expansion in templates.

functions.name: devious.test.test
documentation: Run tests for a target. Without specifying target, all tests are run.

modules.name: devious.targets.django_app.config.debug_settings
documentation:

methods.name: devious.targets.target.Target.create
documentation: Create directory structure for target and register it where necessary.

methods.name: devious.targets.target.Target.test
documentation: Run tests for target. Returning non-zero indicates failure.

methods.name: devious.targets.target.Target.verify
documentation:
Check if target is consistent with model, return True (non-zero) if not.
If implementing additional checks in subclasses, call super first.

functions.name: devious.verify.verify
documentation: Verify consistency of a target and of global config. Without specifying target, all targets are verified.

functions.name: devious.targets.microservice.microservice.copy_files_with_substitution
documentation: Copy a file with string substitution.

classes.name: devious.targets.microservice.microservice.Microservice
documentation:
A microservice is a single docker container that can be deployed to a VM.
It is expected that the VM runs an NGINX instance.
Reverse-proxying requests to the service is configured on deploy.
If no NGINX is installed on the deployment VM, it is installed.

functions.name: devious.targets.django_app.django_app.copy_files_with_substitution
documentation: Copy a file with string substitution.

methods.name: devious.targets.django_app.django_app.DjangoApp.build
documentation: Build django app as Docker container.

functions.name: devious.wrappers.pytest.test_directory
documentation: Test directory with pytest and indicate failure with True (non-zero), optional coverage.

functions.name: devious.update.update
documentation:
Update dev environment with latest changes from devious repository.

Needs to be used initially to decouple the devious upstream.
The update is a simple file copy from a temporary cloned git repository.
It can only add and update but not delete files.

functions.name: devious.install.docker
documentation: Install docker if not installed yet.

modules.name: devious.config
documentation: Custom configuration of utilities like logging.

classes.name: devious.config.RepoConfig
documentation: Context object class for click.ContextObject obj attribute.
